title: OP Client Profile (Rev 1)
description: Frozen Rev-1 contract for an OP Client's profile. One profile per install, committed at repo root as profile.json, served publicly at /profile and /profile.json. Contains NO secrets by construction: credentials/tokens/passwords live only in .env and are resolved at point-of-use by `provider`. See issues #215 (epic) and #216 (Rev 1), and docs/plans/point7/2026-07-02-profile-vocabulary-decoupling.md. Field buckets: A. behavior-gating (relay, indexing*, registrationPolicy, *Harmonizer(s), *Publisher(s), vocabularyDocument, contentLabels, externalAccounts, vocabulary) — inert in Rev 1, wired in Rev 2 (#217); B. declarative identity (name, description, favicon, badge, avatar, terms, contacts, commercialActivity) — only /profile renders them; C. secrets — .env only, never here.

relay.description: This relay's canonical URI. LOADER-RESOLVED: the committed profile MUST set this to null; the C2 loader (packages/core/profile.js) fills it from .env `instance` at load time so the value is never duplicated across the file and .env. Stored as null here to keep the file the single non-secret source of truth without owning the relay URL.

indexingMode.description: Whether the relay actively crawls/indexes or only indexes on explicit request.

indexingFrequency.description: Advisory cadence for active indexing. Free-form label (e.g. 'hourly', 'daily'); semantics finalized in Rev 2.

registrationPolicy.description: Who may register with this relay.

defaultHarmonizer.description: Name of the harmonizer/handler mode used when none is specified (mirrors .env default_handler in Rev 1; wired from profile in Rev 2).

namedHarmonizers.description: Additional harmonizers this client exposes by name. Empty in Rev 1.

defaultPublisher.description: Name of the publisher used when none is specified.

namedPublishers.description: Additional publishers this client exposes by name. Empty in Rev 1.

vocabularyDocument.description: URI of this client's vocabulary document (#194). The canonical core vocab lives in core; this points at the client's additions.

contentLabels.description: Content labels this client declares (#192). Empty in Rev 1.

externalAccounts.description: Accounts the relay itself posts to. PUBLIC side only: { provider, handle }. Credentials are NEVER here — the loader resolves them from .env keyed by `provider` at point-of-use (#216).

provider.description: Stable provider key used to look up credentials in .env at point-of-use.

description.description: Short human-readable description of the client.

badge.description: OP badge image URL or site-relative path (mirrors .env badge_image).

avatar.description: Avatar image URL or site-relative path.

terms.description: URI of the client's terms of service.

contacts.description: Contacts for admins/mods, distinct from externalAccounts.

commercialActivity.description: Whether the client engages in commercial activity.

vocabulary.description: The client's vocabulary additions. Two independent arrays with opposite declaration semantics (see 'The subtype / documentRecord asymmetry' in the decoupling doc): relationshipSubtypes declare TO BE PROMOTED (optional, opt-in first-class path); documentRecord entries declare TO BE ADMITTED AT ALL (required to store + type + project).

relationshipSubtypes.description: Traversable custom edge types promoted to first-class API paths (#236). Undeclared subtypes still flow into the octothorpes array and are reachable ad-hoc via ?st= (#200); declaring one mints /get/<path>/<by> routes. May be empty.

type.description: The subtype class name, stored as octo:<type> (e.g. 'AliasOf', 'Item').

label.description: Human-readable label for generated context/vocab docs (e.g. 'is an alias of').

path.description: URL path segment for the minted routes (e.g. 'aliasesOf' -> /get/aliasesOf/posted).

documentRecord.description: Non-canonical LEAF predicates admitted into blobject.documentRecord (#237). This list is the admission allowlist AND the typing contract: only declared predicates are stored/projected (undeclared harmonizer output is dropped, the #166 abuse guard), and `range` drives the value's JSON type in and out. documentRecord is never traversed by the relationship engine. May be empty (then no documentRecord key is projected).

namespace.description: Namespace prefix the predicate belongs to (e.g. 'schema', 'memex'). Resolved to an IRI by the vocabulary/context layer.

range.description: Value type for storage typing and read-side projection: literal->string, uri->string (semantically a node), timestamp->ISO-8601 string, number->JSON number, boolean->JSON boolean. Enum matches the projection plan (docs/plans/point7/2026-07-07-documentrecord-projection.md).
